title: Brandenburg Fishing License Exam - Question Pool
description: Fragenpool zur Sachkundeprüfung Angelfischerei Brandenburg. Antwort A ist immer die korrekte Antwort im Original-PDF
note: Extracted from PDF. Sections I-IV have 120 questions each

questions.question: Die meisten einheimischen Süßwasserfische gehören zu den ...?
questions.questionEn: Most native freshwater fish belong to the...?
questions.explanation: The vast majority of freshwater fish are bony fish (teleosts). Cartilaginous fish like sharks are almost exclusively marine, and cyclostomes like lampreys are a very small minority

questions.question: Wozu dienen die Barteln?
questions.questionEn: What is the purpose of barbels?

answersEn.A: As a taste and touch organ

questions.explanation: Barbels are sensory organs with taste buds and touch receptors that help fish locate food. They are not used for movement or as feeding appendages

questions.question: Augen sind wichtig für den Beutefang. Bei welcher Fischart besonders?
questions.questionEn: Eyes are important for prey capture. In which fish species especially?
questions.explanation: Pike are primarily sight-based ambush predators. Eels and catfish rely more on smell and barbels, especially in murky or dark waters

questions.question: Wo befindet sich das Herz beim Fisch?
questions.questionEn: Where is the heart located in a fish?

answersEn.C: At the rear part of the swim bladder

questions.explanation: The fish heart is located ventrally in the throat region, just behind the gills. It is positioned well below the kidney

questions.question: Wie werden die Atmungsorgane unserer heimischen Fische bezeichnet?
questions.questionEn: The respiratory organs of our native fish are called?
questions.explanation: Fish breathe through gills that extract dissolved oxygen. Fish do not have lungs, and the swim bladder is a buoyancy organ, not respiratory

questions.question: Welche Flosse ist das Hauptantriebsorgan der Fische bezeichnet?
questions.questionEn: Which fin is the main propulsion organ of fish?
questions.explanation: The caudal (tail) fin provides primary forward thrust. Pelvic and pectoral fins are for steering, braking, and fine movement

questions.question: Was haben alle Salmoniden gemeinsam?
questions.questionEn: What do all salmonids have in common?
questions.explanation: All salmonids possess an adipose fin between dorsal fin and tail. Subterminal mouths are typical of bottom-feeding cyprinids, and barbels characterize carp

questions.question: Der Karpfen gehört zu den ...?
questions.questionEn: The carp belongs to the...?
questions.explanation: Carp are teleosts (bony fish), which make up the vast majority of freshwater fish. The other options represent much smaller, primarily marine or primitive groups

questions.question: Das Neunauge gehört zu den ...?
questions.questionEn: The lamprey belongs to the...?
questions.explanation: Lampreys are jawless cyclostomes with a circular sucking mouth and horny teeth, distinct from both bony and cartilaginous fish

questions.question: Die Schleimhaut eines Fisches ..

answers.A: schützt den Fisch vor äußeren Einflüssen
answers.C: sorgt für die Färbung des Fisches

questions.questionEn: The mucous membrane (slime coat) of a fish..

answersEn.A: protects the fish from external influences
answersEn.C: is responsible for the fish's coloration

questions.explanation: The slime coat protects against pathogens, parasites, and osmotic stress. Scales are embedded in skin, and coloration comes from pigment cells

questions.question: Wozu dient dem Fisch die Seitenlinie?
questions.questionEn: What purpose does the lateral line serve for fish?
questions.explanation: The lateral line detects vibrations, pressure changes, and water movement. It is not for oxygen absorption, which is the gills' function

questions.question: Woher haben die Neunaugen ihren Namen?

answers.A: Beim erwachsenen Tier werden je Körperseite sieben Kiemenöffnungen und die Nasenöffnung dem echten Augen zugerechnet
answers.B: jeder Seite über acht Die Tiere verfügen auf Pigmentflecken und ein Auge
answers.C: Die Tiere besitzen insgesamt neun Pigmentflecken

questions.questionEn: Where do lampreys (Neunaugen) get their name?

answersEn.A: Seven gill openings per side plus nostril and eye counted as 'nine eyes'
answersEn.B: Eight pigment spots and one eye per side

questions.explanation: The name 'Neunauge' (nine-eye) comes from counting seven gill openings plus the nostril and actual eye on each side

questions.question: Worin unterscheidet sich die männliche von der weiblichen Äsche ?

answers.A: Die männliche Äsche besitzt eine längere Rückenflosse
answers.B: Die männliche Äsche besitzt keine Brustflossen

questions.questionEn: How does the male grayling differ from the female?

answersEn.A: The male has a longer dorsal fin
answersEn.B: The male has no pectoral fins

questions.explanation: Male grayling develop a conspicuously larger dorsal fin, especially during spawning. Both sexes have pectoral fins, and grayling lack jaw teeth

questions.question: Wie gut ist der Geruchssinn bei dem Aal ausgeprägt?
questions.questionEn: How well developed is the sense of smell in the eel?
questions.explanation: Eels have an exceptionally acute sense of smell, used for long-distance migration navigation and locating food in dark or murky waters

questions.question: Was versteht man unter einem Laichhaken?

answers.A: Hakenartige Verformung des Unterkiefers bei Milchnern der Lachsartigen
answers.B: Hilfsgerät zum Anlanden von laichreifen Fischen

questions.questionEn: What is a spawning hook (Laichhaken)?

answersEn.A: A hook-shaped deformation of the lower jaw in male salmonids
answersEn.B: A device for landing spawning-ready fish
answersEn.C: A device for removing unwanted spawning nests

questions.explanation: The Laichhaken (kype) is a natural secondary sexual characteristic in male salmonids used in combat, not an artificial tool

questions.question: Welche Fische haben keinen Magen?
questions.questionEn: Which fish have no stomach?
questions.explanation: Crucian carp and tench, like all cyprinids, are stomachless with a long intestine instead. Predatory species like pike possess a true stomach

questions.question: Wieviel Bartfäden hat die Schleie?
questions.questionEn: How many barbels does the tench have?
questions.explanation: The tench has exactly two small barbels at the mouth corners. Carp have four, and many cyprinids have none

questions.question: Eingebettet in der Leber liegt ... ?
questions.questionEn: Embedded in the liver lies...?
questions.explanation: The gallbladder is embedded within liver tissue. The stomach is a separate organ further forward, and the kidney lies along the spine

questions.question: Die Besamung der Eier des Karpfens erfolgt ... ?
questions.questionEn: Fertilization of carp eggs occurs...?
questions.explanation: Carp are oviparous with external fertilization. The female releases eggs and male releases milt simultaneously in the water

questions.question: Welche Fischart hat relativ kleine Augen?
questions.questionEn: Which fish species has relatively small eyes?
questions.explanation: The Wels catfish has disproportionately small eyes, relying on barbels and smell. Roach and rudd are sight-oriented with larger eyes

questions.question: Welche Fischart hat eine pfeilförmige Körperform?
questions.questionEn: Which fish species has an arrow-shaped body?
questions.explanation: Pike have a torpedo/arrow-shaped body for rapid acceleration. Roach are laterally compressed, and eels are snake-like

questions.question: Welche Fischart hat eine Fettflosse?
questions.questionEn: Which fish species has an adipose fin?
questions.explanation: Rainbow trout, like all salmonids, possess an adipose fin. Bitterling (cyprinid) and perch (percid) do not

questions.question: Welche Fischart hat eine Fettflosse?
questions.questionEn: Which fish species has an adipose fin?
questions.explanation: The brown bullhead is an ictalurid catfish with an adipose fin. European Wels catfish and burbot lack this feature

questions.question: Welche Fischart lebt in der hauptsächlichen Abwachszeit im Süßwasser und laicht im Meer?
questions.questionEn: Which fish lives mainly in freshwater and spawns in the sea?
questions.explanation: Eels are catadromous: they grow in freshwater and migrate to the Sargasso Sea to spawn. Salmon are the opposite (anadromous)

questions.question: Welche Fischart lebt in der hauptsächlichen Abwachszeit im Meer un laicht im Süßwasser?
questions.questionEn: Which fish lives mainly in the sea and spawns in freshwater?
questions.explanation: Salmon are anadromous, growing at sea and spawning in rivers. Bullhead and barbel are purely freshwater species

questions.question: Welcher cyprinidenartige Fisch ist im ausgewachsenen Alter ein Raubfisch?
questions.questionEn: Which cyprinid fish is a predator in adulthood?
questions.explanation: The asp is a predatory cyprinid hunting smaller fish. Carp and tench are primarily bottom-feeding omnivores

questions.question: Welche Fischart besitzt Stachelstrahlen in der ersten Rückenflosse?
questions.questionEn: Which fish has spiny rays in the first dorsal fin?
questions.explanation: Perch have a spiny first dorsal fin. Trout have soft-rayed fins, and vendace are coregonids without dorsal spines

questions.question: Welche der genannten Arten besitzt keine Schuppen?
questions.questionEn: Which of these species has no scales?
questions.explanation: Brook lamprey are cyclostomes without scales. Sunbleak and chub are cyprinids with fully developed scales

questions.question: Welche der genannten Arten hat einen Saugmund mit Hornzähnen?
questions.questionEn: Which species has a suction mouth with horny teeth?
questions.explanation: Lampreys have a circular suction mouth lined with horny teeth. Spined loach and gudgeon have typical fish mouths

questions.question: Welche Fische rauben hauptsächlich nachts, wenn das Sehvermögen eine nicht so große Rolle spielt?
questions.questionEn: Which fish hunt mainly at night when eyesight plays a minor role?
questions.explanation: Wels catfish and eels are nocturnal predators relying on smell and barbels. Pike and zander are more visually oriented

questions.question: Welche Fischart besitzt einen durch Verbindung von Afterflosse und Rückenflosse entstandenen Flossensaum?
questions.questionEn: Which fish has a fin seam from connected anal and dorsal fins?
questions.explanation: Eels have a continuous fin seam (dorsal, caudal, anal merged). Brown bullhead and loach have separate distinct fins

questions.question: Bei welcher im Süßwasser lebenden Fischart trägt das Männchen ei überwiegend leuchtend rotes Hochzeitskleid?
questions.questionEn: Which freshwater male fish wears a bright red breeding dress?
questions.explanation: Male three-spined sticklebacks develop brilliant red bellies during breeding. Perch have red fins year-round, and redfish are marine

questions.question: Welcher Fisch legt seine Eier in Muscheln ab?
questions.questionEn: Which fish lays its eggs in mussels?
questions.explanation: The bitterling deposits eggs into freshwater mussel gill cavities via a long ovipositor. Sticklebacks build nests, and smelt spawn over gravel

questions.question: Welcher Fisch besitzt während der Laichzeit giftigen Rogen?
questions.questionEn: Which fish has poisonous roe during spawning season?
questions.explanation: Barbel roe is toxic during spawning and can cause illness. Carp and pike roe are not poisonous when properly prepared

questions.question: Welcher Fisch kann bei Sauerstoffmangel durch den Darm zusätzlich Sauerstoff aufnehmen?
questions.questionEn: Which fish absorbs extra oxygen through its intestine when oxygen is low?
questions.explanation: The weather loach uses its intestine as an accessory respiratory organ, gulping air at the surface. Minnows and gudgeon lack this adaptation

questions.question: Wie unterscheidet sich der "Zwergwels" vom "Europäischen Wels"?

answers.B: Die Schwanzflosse des Zwergwelses ist größe

questions.questionEn: How does the brown bullhead differ from the European Wels?

answersEn.A: The brown bullhead has an adipose fin
answersEn.B: The bullhead's tail fin is larger
answersEn.C: The bullhead has no swim bladder

questions.explanation: The brown bullhead (ictalurid) has an adipose fin; the European Wels catfish does not. Both have swim bladders

questions.question: Welcher Fisch im Land Brandenburg lebt im Freiwasser tiefer, klarer Seen?
questions.questionEn: Which Brandenburg fish lives in the open water of deep, clear lakes?
questions.explanation: Vendace are pelagic fish of deep, cold, clear lakes. Tench prefer warm weedy shallows, and carp thrive in warmer eutrophic waters

questions.question: Welche Fischart bevorzugt nährstoffreiche Gewässer?
questions.questionEn: Which fish species prefers nutrient-rich waters?
questions.explanation: Carp thrive in eutrophic warm waters. Brown trout and bullhead require cool, oxygen-rich, oligotrophic conditions

questions.question: Zu welcher Ordnung gehört die Quappe?
questions.questionEn: To which order does the burbot belong?
questions.explanation: The burbot is the only freshwater member of the cod family (Gadiformes), sharing characteristics like a chin barbel with marine cod

questions.question: Welche Fischarten zählen zu den Laichräubern?
questions.questionEn: Which fish species are spawning predators (Laichraeuber)?
questions.explanation: Eels and burbot actively prey on fish eggs and spawning congregations. Pike and zander prey on adult fish rather than eggs

questions.question: Welche Fischart verteidigt ihren Laichplatz?
questions.questionEn: Which fish species defends its spawning site?
questions.explanation: Zander (pikeperch) guard their nests and defend spawning sites until eggs hatch. Pike and carp provide no parental care

questions.question: Zu der Familie der Karpfenfische (Cyprinidae) gehören ...?
questions.questionEn: The carp family (Cyprinidae) includes...?
questions.explanation: Gudgeon, chub, and rudd are true cyprinids. Weather loach and related species belong to the loach family (Cobitidae)

questions.question: Zu der Familie der Lachse (Salmonidae) gehören ...?
questions.questionEn: The salmon family (Salmonidae) includes...?
questions.explanation: Brown trout and brook char are salmonids. Zander, ruffe, and perch are percids; gudgeon, barbel, and minnow are cyprinids

questions.question: Wie ist die Maulstellung beim Döbel?
questions.questionEn: What is the mouth position of the chub?
questions.explanation: Chub have a terminal mouth at the snout tip for mid-water feeding. Inferior mouths indicate bottom feeders, superior mouths surface feeders

questions.question: Welcher einheimische Fisch kann sein Maul rüsselartig vorstülpen?
questions.questionEn: Which native fish can protrude its mouth like a trunk?
questions.explanation: Carp have highly protrusible mouths extended like a trunk to suck up bottom food. Vendace have limited protrusion, and pike jaws are fixed

questions.question: Welcher Fisch hat einen gekielten Bauch?
questions.questionEn: Which fish has a keeled belly?
questions.explanation: Rudd have a sharp keeled belly ridge behind the pelvic fins for hydrodynamics. Burbot and trout have rounded bellies

questions.question: Bei welcher Fischart ist die Schwanzflosse gerundet?
questions.questionEn: In which fish is the tail fin rounded?
questions.explanation: Burbot have a rounded caudal fin. Eels have a continuous fin seam, and zander have a forked tail

questions.question: Welche Fischart ist hochrückig?
questions.questionEn: Which fish species is deep-bodied (high-backed)?
questions.explanation: Bream have a distinctly deep, laterally compressed body. Chub and barbel are more elongated or cylindrical

questions.question: Welche Flosse hat keine Flossenstrahlen?
questions.questionEn: Which fin has no fin rays?

answersEn.A: The adipose fin of the brown trout
answersEn.C: The dorsal fin of European perch

questions.explanation: The adipose fin is fleshy and rayless. Zander and perch dorsal fins contain true fin rays (spiny or soft)

questions.question: Welcher Fisch hat einen geringen Fettgehalt im Fleisch?
questions.questionEn: Which fish has low fat content in its meat?
questions.explanation: Pike have very lean, low-fat white meat. Carp have moderate fat, and eels are notably fatty fish

questions.question: Welcher Fisch betreibt Brutpflege?
questions.questionEn: Which fish practices brood care?
questions.explanation: Male bullheads guard and fan their eggs until hatching. Carp and vendace are broadcast spawners with no parental care

questions.question: Welche Fischart besitzt keine Stachelstrahlen im ersten Teil der Rückenflosse?
questions.questionEn: Which fish has no spiny rays in the first dorsal fin?
questions.explanation: Pike have only soft dorsal rays. Zander and perch both have distinctly spiny first dorsal fins

questions.question: Welcher Fisch riecht nach frischer grüner Gurke?
questions.questionEn: Which fish smells like fresh green cucumber?
questions.explanation: Smelt have a distinctive cucumber-like scent, well-known among anglers. This odor is unique to smelt among freshwater fish

questions.question: Welche Fischart ist anfällig für die Bauchwassersucht?
questions.questionEn: Which fish is susceptible to abdominal dropsy?
questions.explanation: Carp are particularly prone to infectious abdominal dropsy (ascites), a bacterial disease well-known in carp aquaculture

questions.question: Wie äußert sich die Grießkörnchenkrankheit?

answers.A: Durch weiße Punkte auf em ganzen Körper
answers.B: Durch weiße Punkte auf der Leber

questions.questionEn: How does white spot disease (ich) manifest?

answersEn.A: Through white spots on the entire body
answersEn.B: Through white spots on the liver

questions.explanation: Ichthyophthirius causes visible white spots on skin and fins, like grains of semolina. It affects external surfaces, not internal organs

questions.question: Wo schmarotzen Karpfenläuse?
questions.questionEn: Where do carp lice parasitize?
questions.explanation: Carp lice (Argulus) are external parasites on skin and fins over the entire body. They are not restricted to gills or internal organs

questions.question: Was ist eine Karpfenlaus?

answers.A: Ein Kleinkrebs, der auf der Haut von Karpfenfischen

questions.questionEn: What is a carp louse?

answersEn.A: A small crustacean on cyprinid skin that sucks blood

questions.explanation: The carp louse (Argulus) is a parasitic crustacean feeding on blood, causing irritation and secondary infections. It is not food

questions.question: Welche Krankheit ist für den Karpfen typisch ?
questions.questionEn: Which disease is typical for carp?
questions.explanation: Abdominal dropsy (infectious ascites) is a characteristic carp disease. Redmouth affects salmonids, and cauliflower disease affects eels

questions.question: Im Mai fange ich einen Blei mit weißen "Körnchen" auf dem Kopf. Worum handelt es sich mit hoher Wahrscheinlichkeit?
questions.questionEn: In May I catch a bream with white 'granules' on the head. What is this?
questions.explanation: Many cyprinids develop temporary white breeding tubercles (spawning rash) during the spawning season. This is hormonal, not pathological

questions.question: Was ist in der Fischerei unter dem Begriff "Messerrücken" zu verstehen?

answers.A: Hungerform von Karpfenfischen, speziell beim Blei

questions.questionEn: What is 'knife-back' (Messerruecken) in fisheries?

answersEn.A: Starvation form of cyprinids, especially bream

questions.explanation: Messerruecken describes the emaciated, sharply keeled back of starving cyprinids in overpopulated waters, indicating poor body condition

questions.question: Wo parasitieren Fischegel?
questions.questionEn: Where do fish leeches parasitize?
questions.explanation: Fish leeches (Piscicola) are external parasites on skin and fins. They are not internal parasites

questions.question: Worauf ist beim Kauf von Satzschleien und vor ihrem Aussatz besonders zu achten?

answers.C: Auf günstige Anteile von Rognern und Milchnern

questions.questionEn: What to check when buying stocking tench?
questions.explanation: Ergasilus (gill lice) infestation is the primary health concern when stocking tench, as it can severely impact stocked fish

questions.question: Welche Maßnahmen können das seuchenbiologische Gleichgewicht im Gewässer beeinträchtigen?

answers.A: Aussetzen gebietsfremder Fische, Einleitung organischer Abwässer
answers.B: Starke Beangelung, Entnahme von Weißfischen beim Hegefischen
answers.C: Rohrernte im Winter, Fischereiausübung mit der Spinnangel im Sommer

questions.questionEn: What can disrupt the epidemiological balance in a water body?

answersEn.A: Stocking non-native fish, organic wastewater discharge
answersEn.B: Heavy angling, removing coarse fish during management fishing

questions.explanation: Non-native fish introduce new pathogens, and organic pollution stresses fish immunity. Normal management activities are not primary disruptors

questions.question: Was ruft bei der sogenannten Drehkrankheit die Drehbewegung hervor?

answers.B: Versuche der Fische zur Abstoßung der Parasiten

questions.questionEn: What causes the whirling in whirling disease?

answersEn.A: Parasite spore deposits in the balance organ

questions.explanation: Whirling disease (Myxobolus cerebralis) damages the balance organ and nervous system. It is not from behavioral attempts to shake off parasites

questions.question: Zu den Außenparasiten zählen ...?
questions.questionEn: External parasites include...?
questions.explanation: Fish leeches are ectoparasites on external surfaces. Tapeworms and nematodes are internal parasites

questions.question: Wodurch kann es zu Verpilzungen kommen?
questions.questionEn: What can cause fungal infections in fish?
questions.explanation: Saprolegnia fungus typically infects wounds and damaged skin as a secondary infection. Primary fungal infection without prior injury is rare

questions.question: Weshalb ist in vielen Gewässern Brandenburgs Aalbesatz notwendig?

answers.C: Wegen der zu starken Beangelung des Hechtes

questions.questionEn: Why is eel stocking necessary in many Brandenburg waters?

answersEn.A: Lack of juvenile eel upstream migration

questions.explanation: Dams and weirs block natural glass eel migration from the sea. Without stocking, eels cannot recolonize inland waters

questions.question: Wie wird Hechtbesatz getätigt?

answers.A: Einzeln in Ufernähe, wenn möglich über Krautbänken

questions.questionEn: How is pike stocking carried out?

answersEn.A: Individually near bank, preferably over weed beds
answersEn.C: In small quantities in open water

questions.explanation: Pike fry should be stocked individually in vegetated shallows to establish territories and avoid cannibalism. Concentrated stocking increases losses

questions.question: Die Quappenbestände im Land Brandenburg sind stark zurückgegangen. Weshalb?

answers.A: Die Vielzahl der Wehre versperrt die Wege zu den Laichgebieten
answers.B: Sie finden zu wenig Futter. Sie werden zu stark

questions.questionEn: Why have burbot populations in Brandenburg declined?

answersEn.A: Weirs block access to spawning grounds

questions.explanation: Burbot require access to upstream spawning areas. Weirs block these winter spawning migrations, causing population collapse

questions.question: Wie kann ich Zanderbestände ohne Besatz in einem See fördern?

answers.C: Anbringen von künstlichen Lichtquellen an den Laichplätzen

questions.questionEn: How to promote zander stocks without stocking?
questions.explanation: Zander are nest-spawners benefiting from artificial spawning nests (brushed structures). This improves natural reproduction without stocking

questions.question: Welche Naturnahrung bevorzugt der Karpfen?
questions.questionEn: Which natural food does carp prefer?
questions.explanation: Carp primarily feed on benthic chironomid larvae (bloodworms). They are not typically piscivorous and rarely eat surface insects

questions.question: Wo fressen Fische mit unterständigem Maul vorwiegend?
questions.questionEn: Where do fish with subterminal mouths primarily feed?
questions.explanation: An inferior (unterstaendig) mouth is adapted for bottom feeding. Superior mouths are for surface feeding

questions.question: Welche Fischnährtiere leben in der Bodenzone?
questions.questionEn: Which food organisms live in the bottom zone?
questions.explanation: Red chironomid larvae (bloodworms) live in sediment. Water fleas and copepods are planktonic organisms in the water column

questions.question: Fischbrut ernährt sich vorwiegend..
questions.questionEn: Fish fry primarily feed on..
questions.explanation: Fish fry initially feed on zooplankton (rotifers, copepods) because their mouths are too small for larger prey

questions.question: Was wird auch als Sprock bezeichnet?
questions.questionEn: What is also called 'Sprock'?
questions.explanation: Sprock is a regional German angler term for caddisfly larvae (Koecherfliegenlarven), which build protective cases

questions.question: Wann benötigt ein Fisch mehr Sauerstoff?

answers.C: Er verbraucht immer gleich viel Sauerstoff

questions.questionEn: When does a fish need more oxygen?
questions.explanation: Fish are ectothermic: metabolic rate increases with temperature, raising oxygen demand. Warm water also holds less dissolved oxygen

questions.question: Wie entstehen nach heutigem Erkenntnisstand die Aalformen "Breitkopf" und "Spitzkopf"?

answers.A: Durch das vorhandene Nahrungsangebot im Gewässer

questions.questionEn: How do eel forms 'broad-head' and 'pointed-head' arise?
questions.explanation: Eel head morphology is phenotypically plastic: broad heads develop when eating larger prey (fish), narrow heads when eating invertebrates

questions.question: Welche der Fischarten ernährt sich ausschließlich von Plankton?
questions.questionEn: Which fish feeds exclusively on plankton?
questions.explanation: Vendace are obligate planktivores filtering zooplankton. Gudgeon are bottom-feeders, and carp are omnivorous benthivores

questions.question: Bei welcher Wassertemperatur ist der Karpfen am fressfreudigsten?
questions.questionEn: At what temperature is carp most eager to feed?
questions.explanation: Carp are warm-water fish with optimal feeding at 20-25C. Below 12C their metabolism slows significantly

questions.question: Warum haben gute Aalgewässer oftmals einen schlechten Krebsbestand?

answers.B: Der Aal bevorzugt die gleiche Nahrung wie der Kreb
answers.C: Der Aal ist der Überträger der Krebspest

questions.questionEn: Why do good eel waters have poor crayfish populations?

answersEn.B: Eels prefer the same food as crayfish

questions.explanation: Eels are significant crayfish predators. High eel populations decimate crayfish through direct predation, not competition or disease

questions.question: Welche der Fischarten neigen bei Massenaufkommen zur "Verbuttung"? (Kleinwüchsigkeit mit Laichreife)
questions.questionEn: Which fish tend towards stunting (Verbuttung) in mass populations?
questions.explanation: Crucian carp are notorious for stunting in overpopulated waters, maturing at very small sizes. Zander and trout are less prone

questions.question: Was ist Plankton?

answers.A: Die Gesamtheit der im Freiwasserraum lebenden, mit der Wasserbewegung passiv treibenden Organismen
answers.B: Die Gesamtheit der Schwimmblattwasserpflan zen eines Gewässers
answers.C: Die Gesamtheit der Zuckmückenlarven, Gelbrandkäfer und Libellenlarven eines Gewässers

questions.questionEn: What is plankton?

answersEn.A: Organisms drifting passively in open water
answersEn.C: Midge larvae, diving beetles, and dragonfly larvae

questions.explanation: Plankton comprises all drifting organisms (phyto- and zooplankton). Aquatic plants and insect larvae are benthic or nektonic, not planktonic

questions.question: Welche der Fischarten benötigen kühleres Wasser?
questions.questionEn: Which fish species require cooler water?
questions.explanation: Trout and brook char are cold-water salmonids needing temperatures below 20C. Tench, eels, carp, and roach tolerate warmer water

questions.question: Welche der Fischarten benötigen einen hohen Sauerstoffgehalt im Wasser?
questions.questionEn: Which fish need high oxygen levels?
questions.explanation: Salmonids require high dissolved oxygen (>=7 mg/L). Roach, eels, carp, and tench tolerate much lower levels

questions.question: Welche Fische nehmen vorwiegend Oberflächennahrung auf?
questions.questionEn: Which fish take primarily surface food?
questions.explanation: Superior (oberstaendig) mouths are adapted for surface feeding. Terminal mouths for mid-water, inferior for bottom feeding

questions.question: Die Entwicklung der Fischeier des Karpfens ..

answers.A: verkürzt sich mit steigender Temperatur im Toleranzbereich
answers.B: verkürzt sich mit sinkender Temperatur im Toleranzbereich
answers.C: hängt aufgrund des fehlenden Temperaturempfindens der Temperatur ab

questions.questionEn: Carp egg development..

answersEn.A: accelerates with rising temperature in the tolerance range

questions.explanation: Fish egg development is temperature-dependent. Higher temperatures accelerate embryonic development within the species' tolerance range

questions.question: Welche der Fischarten frisst oftmals Wasserpflanzen?
questions.questionEn: Which fish often eats aquatic plants?
questions.explanation: Rudd are omnivorous and frequently consume soft aquatic plants. Sea trout are piscivorous/insectivorous, and ruffe eat benthic invertebrates

questions.question: Wie hoch ist die Körpertemperatur der Fische?
questions.questionEn: What is the body temperature of fish?
questions.explanation: Fish are ectothermic poikilotherms: their body temperature matches the surrounding water. They cannot maintain a different temperature

questions.question: Was ist ein Milchner?
questions.questionEn: What is a 'Milchner'?
questions.explanation: Milchner is the German term for a male fish (producing milt). A female is a Rogner (from roe/eggs)

questions.question: Warum gibt es Schonzeiten?

answers.A: Um den Fischen eine ungestörte Laichablage zu ermöglichen
answers.C: Um zu verhindern, dass zu viele Fische auf den Markt kommen

questions.questionEn: Why are there closed seasons (Schonzeiten)?

answersEn.C: To prevent too many fish reaching market

questions.explanation: Closed seasons protect fish during reproduction, allowing them to spawn without disturbance for sustainable populations

questions.question: Was ist unter dem Begriff Laichausschlag zu verstehen?

answers.A: Ausschlag bei einigen Fischarten zur Laichzeit
answers.C: Ausschlag bei einigen Fischarten nach der Laichzeit

questions.questionEn: What is spawning rash (Laichausschlag)?

answersEn.A: Epidermal rash on some fish during spawning

questions.explanation: Spawning rash describes temporary hormonal breeding tubercles on cyprinids during spawning. It is a normal phenomenon, not a disease

questions.question: Was versteht man unter einem Butterkrebs?
questions.questionEn: What is a 'Butterkrebs' (butter crayfish)?
questions.explanation: A Butterkrebs has just molted and its new shell is still soft. It hides until the exoskeleton hardens

questions.question: Wo entwickeln sich die Eier vom Krebs?

answers.A: An der Unterseite des Hinterleibes des weiblichen Krebses

questions.questionEn: Where do crayfish eggs develop?

answersEn.A: On the underside of the female's abdomen

questions.explanation: Female crayfish carry fertilized eggs attached to swimmerets under the tail until hatching. This is characteristic of all freshwater crayfish

questions.question: Welche hier genannten Körperteile und Merkmale werden zur Altersbestimmung von Fischen genutzt?

answers.A: Anzahl der Ringe auf Schuppen und Otolithen (Steine aus dem Gleichgewichtsorgan)

questions.questionEn: What is used to determine fish age?

answersEn.A: Rings on scales and otoliths (ear stones)
answersEn.C: Number and arrangement of dorsal fin rays

questions.explanation: Annual growth rings on scales and otoliths are counted like tree rings. Intermuscular bones and fin rays are unreliable for aging

questions.question: Was ist hinsichtlich der Körperlänge weiblicher und männlicher Aale richtig?
questions.questionEn: Regarding body length of female vs male eels:
questions.explanation: Female eels grow significantly larger (over 1m), while males rarely exceed 50-60cm. This sexual dimorphism is pronounced

questions.question: Wie wird die Totallänge des Fisches gemessen?

answers.A: Von der Maulspitze bis zum Ende der natürlich ausgebreiteten Schwanzflosse
answers.B: Von den Brustflossen bis zum Ansatz der Schwanzflosse
answers.C: Von den Kiemendeckeln bis zur Afterflosse

questions.questionEn: How is total fish length measured?

answersEn.A: From snout tip to end of naturally spread tail fin
answersEn.B: From pectoral fins to tail fin base
answersEn.C: From gill covers to anal fin

questions.explanation: Total length is measured from snout/mouth tip to the furthest point of the naturally spread tail fin

questions.question: Was wird unter dem Begriff "saures Wasser" verstanden ?
questions.questionEn: What is 'acidic water'?
questions.explanation: Acidic water has pH below 7. Turbidity measures clarity, not acidity. High pH means alkaline/basic

questions.question: Was versteht man unter einem neutralen pH-Wert?
questions.questionEn: What is a neutral pH value?
questions.explanation: A pH of 7 is chemically neutral. Below 7 is acidic; above 7 is alkaline

questions.question: Können sich die Marmorkarpfen in Brandenburger Gewässern selbst reproduzieren?
questions.questionEn: Can marble carp reproduce in Brandenburg waters?
questions.explanation: Marble carp are sterile triploid hybrids that cannot reproduce naturally. They are specifically bred for non-reproducing stocking

questions.question: Welche Jahreszeit ist Quappenbeißzeit?
questions.questionEn: Which season is burbot feeding time?
questions.explanation: Burbot are cold-water fish most active in winter. They spawn in winter and feed most aggressively during the coldest months

questions.question: Karpfen sind ...?
questions.questionEn: Carp are...?
questions.explanation: Carp spawn from May to July when water reaches 17-20C. They are summer spawners, not spring or winter

questions.question: Gehört der Silberkarpfen zu den schutzbedürftigen heimischen Fischarten?
questions.questionEn: Is silver carp a protected native fish?
questions.explanation: Silver carp is non-native, introduced from Asia. It is not a protected native Brandenburg species

questions.question: Welche Tiere sind Schädlinge der Fischbrut?
questions.questionEn: Which animals are predators of fish fry?
questions.explanation: Great diving beetle larvae are aggressive predators of fish fry. Water fleas and tubifex worms are fish food, not predators

questions.question: Welche Fischarten sind Gelegelaicher?
questions.questionEn: Which fish are egg-scatterers (Gelegelaicher)?
questions.explanation: Carp and roach scatter adhesive eggs over vegetation. Zander are nest-guarders, eels spawn at sea, and trout are gravel-spawners

questions.question: Welche Fischarten nutzen bevorzugt für die Eiablage in das Wasser ragende Bäume, Äste, totes Geäst usw.?
questions.questionEn: Which fish use trees, branches, dead wood for egg deposition?
questions.explanation: Zander and perch deposit eggs on submerged wood and roots. Pike spawn on flooded vegetation, trout in gravel

questions.question: Welche Fische sind keine heimischen Arten in den Brandenburger Gewässern?
questions.questionEn: Which fish are not native in Brandenburg?

answersEn.A: Grass carp, Marble carp, Silver carp

questions.explanation: Grass, marble, and silver carp are Asian introductions. Crucian carp, ide, bitterling, rudd, and others are native European species

questions.question: Kann an den Schuppen das Alter der Fische abgelesen werden?
questions.questionEn: Can fish age be read from scales?
questions.explanation: Scales develop annual growth rings (annuli) countable like tree rings, valid for both sexes. This is standard fisheries science

questions.question: Wie alt können Karpfen werden?
questions.questionEn: How old can carp get?
questions.explanation: Carp can live up to 40-50 years under favorable conditions. Ten years is typical for many fish, but carp are notably long-lived

questions.question: Welcher Karpfenfisch ist nicht einheimisch?
questions.questionEn: Which carp-like fish is not native?
questions.explanation: Grass carp is native to East Asia and introduced to Europe. Tench and gudgeon are native European cyprinids

questions.question: Aale laichen ...?
questions.questionEn: Eels spawn...?
questions.explanation: European eels are semelparous: they spawn once in the Sargasso Sea and then die

questions.question: Was sind Blankaale?
questions.questionEn: What are 'Blankaale' (silver eels)?
questions.explanation: Blankaale are mature silver eels physiologically transformed (silver color, enlarged eyes) for spawning migration. They are not juveniles

questions.question: Die Schleie ist ein ...?
questions.questionEn: The tench is a...?
questions.explanation: Tench are phytophilous, depositing adhesive eggs on aquatic vegetation. They do not spawn on gravel or bare bottom

questions.question: Welche Fischart erreicht Stückgewichte von mehr als 10 Kilogramm?
questions.questionEn: Which fish reaches individual weights over 10 kg?
questions.explanation: Carp regularly exceed 10 kg. Tench rarely exceed 3-4 kg, and gudgeon are small (<15cm)

questions.question: Wo laicht der Hecht?
questions.questionEn: Where does pike spawn?
questions.explanation: Pike are phytophilous spawners on flooded vegetation in warm shallows. They do not spawn in open water or on gravel

questions.question: Wann laicht der Zander?
questions.questionEn: When does zander spawn?
questions.explanation: Zander spawn in spring (March-June) when water reaches 12-15C. They are not summer or autumn spawners

questions.question: Nach welcher Fischart sind große, flache, trübe Seen mit häufiger Wasserblüten benannt?
questions.questionEn: After which fish are large, shallow, turbid lakes named?
questions.explanation: 'Zander lakes' are large, shallow, eutrophic lakes with high turbidity -- the preferred zander habitat

questions.question: Welche Fischart unternimmt keine Laichwanderung?
questions.questionEn: Which fish does not undertake spawning migrations?
questions.explanation: The nine-spined stickleback is sedentary. Salmon and eels are famous for extensive spawning migrations

questions.question: Wie heißt die in Brandenburg ursprüngliche einheimische Krebsart?
questions.questionEn: What is the original native crayfish in Brandenburg?
questions.explanation: The noble crayfish (Astacus astacus) is the native species. There are no native freshwater lobsters, and American crayfish are invasive

questions.question: Der Bitterling ist ein ... ?
questions.questionEn: The bitterling is a...?
questions.explanation: Bitterling are small schooling cyprinids. They are not solitary or predatory, and the name does not refer to taste

questions.question: Welcher Zehnfußkrebs unternimmt flussaufwärts?
questions.questionEn: Which decapod crustacean undertakes upstream migration?
questions.explanation: The Chinese mitten crab is catadromous with significant upstream migrations. Signal and swamp crayfish are more sedentary

questions.question: Welches der Gewässer ist als sauerstoffreich einzustufen?

answers.A: Ein schnell fließender, kühler und klarer Bach

questions.questionEn: Which water body is oxygen-rich?
questions.explanation: Fast-flowing cool water absorbs more oxygen through turbulence. Stagnant ponds and turbid lakes have lower oxygen due to limited aeration and organic decomposition

questions.question: Moorige Gewässer sind in der Regel artenarm. Warum finden hier nu wenige Pflanzen- und Tierarten ihren Lebensraum?

answers.C: Die Gewässer sind meist sehr tief

questions.questionEn: Why are boggy waters species-poor?

answersEn.C: The waters are usually very deep

questions.explanation: Bog waters have low pH from humic acids, creating an environment too acidic for most aquatic organisms, limiting species diversity

questions.question: Welche Teile von Unterwasserpflanzen sind für die Sauerstofferzeugung von entscheidender Bedeutung?
questions.questionEn: Which parts of underwater plants are crucial for oxygen production?
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis occurs in chlorophyll-containing green leaves. Roots absorb nutrients and stems provide support but contribute little to oxygen production

questions.question: Verbrauchen Unterwasserpflanzen im Dunkeln Sauerstoff?

answers.C: Ja, aber nur bei Wassertemperaturen höher als 20 Grad Celsius

questions.questionEn: Do underwater plants consume oxygen in darkness?

answersEn.C: Yes, but only above 20 degrees Celsius

questions.explanation: Plants respire continuously. Without photosynthesis to offset respiration in darkness, they are net oxygen consumers regardless of temperature

questions.question: In tiefen Seen kann das Licht den Grund nicht mehr erreichen. Wachsen dort Laichkräuter?

answers.A: Nein, zum Wachstum ist Licht notwendig

questions.questionEn: In deep lakes, light cannot reach the bottom. Do pondweeds grow there?

answersEn.A: No, light is necessary for growth

questions.explanation: Aquatic plants need light for photosynthesis. Below the photic zone, insufficient light -- not nutrients -- prevents growth

questions.question: Wie wird die vom Licht nicht erreichbare und von Pflanzen nicht besiedelte Gewässerzone bezeichnet?
questions.questionEn: What is the unlit, plant-free zone called?
questions.explanation: The profundal lies below the photic zone. The littoral is the vegetated shallows, and the pelagic is the open water

questions.question: Der Dorfteich kann leicht mit Schwimmblattpflanzen mit einer Größe von wenigen Millimetern zuwachsen. Es ist die ...?
questions.questionEn: Village ponds overgrown with millimeter-sized floating plants are covered with...?
questions.explanation: Duckweed (Lemna) is a tiny floating plant that rapidly covers pond surfaces. Volvox is colonial algae, and water lilies have much larger leaves

questions.question: Welche der Larven baut sich einen schützenden Behälter aus Pflanzenresten?
questions.questionEn: Which larva builds a protective case from plant material?
questions.explanation: Caddisfly larvae construct protective cases from sand, plant fragments, or shells. Dragonfly and diving beetle larvae are free-living

questions.question: Welche Antwort ist richtig?

answers.A: Die Artenvielfalt der Fische nimmt von der Quelle zur Mündung zu
answers.B: Die Artenvielfalt ist von der Quelle zur Mündung gleich
answers.C: Die Artenvielfalt nimmt von der Quelle zur Mündung ab

questions.questionEn: How does fish species diversity change from source to mouth?
questions.explanation: Following the River Continuum Concept, species diversity increases downstream as rivers become larger, warmer, and more diverse in habitat

questions.question: Wodurch wird die Entwicklung von pflanzlichem Plankton besonders gefördert?
questions.questionEn: What particularly promotes phytoplankton development?
questions.explanation: Phosphorus is typically the limiting nutrient for algal growth in freshwater. Current and depth inhibit rather than promote blooms

questions.question: Was ist ein Teich?

answers.A: Ein ablassbares und gegen den Fischwechsel absperrbares, künstlich angelegtes Gewässer
answers.B: Ein natürlich flacher und kleiner See

questions.questionEn: What is a pond (Teich) in fisheries terminology?

answersEn.A: An artificial, drainable water body blocked against fish migration

questions.explanation: A Teich is specifically artificial and drainable. Natural shallow lakes and oxbows have different characteristics

questions.question: Welche der Insektenlarven ernährt sich auch von kleinen Fischen?
questions.questionEn: Which insect larvae also feed on small fish?
questions.explanation: Great diving beetle larvae are aggressive predators capable of killing small fish. Blackfly and midge larvae are filter-feeders or detritivores

questions.question: Hat die Gestalt des Gewässergrundes fischereibiologische Bedeutung?

answers.A: Ja, da ein unregelmäßiger Untergrund den bodenbesiedelnden Lebewesen vielfältige Entwicklungsmöglichkeiten bietet
answers.C: Ja, da ein unregelmäßiger Bodengrund vor allem Parasiten geeigneten Unterschlupf bietet

questions.questionEn: Does bottom shape have fisheries significance?

answersEn.A: Yes, irregular bottom offers diverse habitats

questions.explanation: Structural diversity provides varied microhabitats for benthic organisms, increasing biodiversity and fish food. The parasite concern is secondary

questions.question: Warum bildet sich in stehenden Gewässern eine Temperaturschichtung?

answers.A: Weil das erwärmte Oberflächenwasser leichter als kälteres Wasser ist
answers.B: Weil das Tiefenwasser durch Erdwärme aufgeheizt wird
answers.C: Weil das erwärmte Wasser schwerer als kälteres Wasser ist

questions.questionEn: Why does thermal stratification form in lakes?

answersEn.A: Warm surface water is lighter than cold water
answersEn.C: Warm water is heavier than cold water

questions.explanation: Water is densest at 4C. Warmer surface water is less dense and floats above colder layers, creating stratification

questions.question: Was ist ein Altarm?
questions.questionEn: What is an oxbow lake (Altarm)?

answersEn.B: A lake area with clean water
answersEn.C: A water body with old fish populations

questions.explanation: An Altarm is a meander cutoff -- a former river channel isolated from the main flow. Not related to water quality or fish age

questions.question: Was benötigen Unterwasserpflanzen, um Sauerstoff zu erzeugen?
questions.questionEn: What do underwater plants need to produce oxygen?
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis requires light energy as the primary enabling factor. Temperature and pH are secondary

questions.question: In welcher Gewässerregion gibt es die meisten Fischarten?
questions.questionEn: Which river region has the most fish species?
questions.explanation: The bream region (lower reaches) is most species-rich due to warmer temperatures, higher nutrients, and diverse habitats

questions.question: Wie soll der Fischbestand in einem Gewässer beschaffen sein?

answers.A: Dem Gewässer entsprechend artenreich und in ausgewogener Alterszusammensetzung
answers.B: Dem Markt entsprechend mit hauptsächlich großen, kapitalen Fischarten
answers.C: Der anglerischen Attraktivität entsprechend mit vorwiegend fangfähigen Fischen

questions.questionEn: How should fish stocks be constituted?

answersEn.A: Species-rich per water type, balanced age structure

questions.explanation: Sustainable management aims for a natural, species-appropriate stock with balanced age classes, not market or angler convenience

questions.question: Was ist unter einer Vollzirkulation eines Sees zu verstehen?

answers.B: Die vollständige Umsetzung der verfügbaren Nährstoffe in pflanzliche Biomasse
answers.C: Der vollständige Sauerstoffabbau im Winter unter der Eisdecke

questions.questionEn: What is full circulation (Vollzirkulation) of a lake?

answersEn.A: Complete mixing of the water body
answersEn.B: Complete conversion of nutrients into biomass

questions.explanation: Full circulation is the seasonal overturn when the entire water column mixes at equalized temperatures, redistributing oxygen and nutrients

questions.question: Wann wird der Sauerstoffgehalt in einem stehenden Gewässer gering sein?

answers.A: Bei Dunkelheit und Massenvorkommen von Algen oder Unterwasserpflanzen
answers.B: Bei Sonnenschein und Massenvorkommen von Algen oder Unterwasserpflanzen
answers.C: Der Sauerstoffgehalt ist in stehenden Gewässern immer gering

questions.questionEn: When is oxygen content low in standing water?

answersEn.A: During darkness with mass algae/plant occurrence
answersEn.B: During sunshine with mass algae/plant occurrence
answersEn.C: Oxygen is always low in standing water

questions.explanation: At night, plants and algae respire but don't photosynthesize, consuming oxygen. Massive blooms can cause severe nocturnal oxygen depletion

questions.question: In wenig abwasserbelasteten Bächen leben bevorzugt ...?
questions.questionEn: Which organisms indicate clean streams?
questions.explanation: Stonefly and mayfly larvae are sensitive bioindicators of good water quality. Tubifex worms indicate organic pollution

questions.question: Wie lautet die richtige Reihenfolge der Regionen im Fließgewässer?
questions.questionEn: What is the correct river region order?
questions.explanation: The classical zonation proceeds from cold headwaters (trout) through grayling, barbel, and bream regions to the estuary

questions.question: Wodurch kann in einem Gewässer der Sauerstoffgehalt schnell abnehmen?

answers.A: Durch Einleitung von Abwasser und starke organische Verschmutzung
answers.B: Durch schnelles Absinken der Wassertemperatur um mehr als 5 Grad Celsius
answers.C: Durch Photosynthese der Wasserpflanzen bei starker Sonneneinstrahlung

questions.questionEn: What causes rapid oxygen decrease?
questions.explanation: Organic pollution triggers microbial decomposition consuming large amounts of oxygen. Temperature drops and photosynthesis increase oxygen

questions.question: Was versteht man unter der Wasserblüte?
questions.questionEn: What is an algal bloom (Wasserbluete)?

answersEn.C: Heavy occurrence of flowering aquatic plants

questions.explanation: An algal bloom is massive proliferation of microscopic algae/cyanobacteria, not pollen or flowering macrophytes

questions.question: Wann tritt die "Vollzirkulation" in einem geschichteten See auf?
questions.questionEn: When does full circulation occur in a stratified lake?
questions.explanation: Full circulation occurs in spring and autumn when surface water reaches 4C (maximum density), enabling complete mixing

questions.question: Welche Fließgewässerregion ist für Brandenburg typisch?
questions.questionEn: Which river region is typical for Brandenburg?
questions.explanation: Brandenburg's lowland rivers are slow-flowing and warm, corresponding to the bream region. Trout regions occur at higher elevations

questions.question: Wie ist ein "Zandersee" beschaffen?

answers.A: Trübes Wasser und ein großer Freiwasserbereich

questions.questionEn: What characterizes a 'zander lake'?

answersEn.A: Turbid water and large open-water area

questions.explanation: Zander lakes are eutrophic with high turbidity and extensive pelagic zones. Shallow weedy lakes favor pike

questions.question: Wie ist ein "Maränensee" beschaffen?
questions.questionEn: What characterizes a 'vendace lake'?
questions.explanation: Vendace require deep, cold, oligotrophic lakes with high water clarity, contrasting sharply with turbid zander lakes

questions.question: Welche Fischarten sind für Niederungsforellenbäche typisch?
questions.questionEn: Which fish are typical for lowland trout streams?
questions.explanation: Lowland trout streams support trout, loaches, bullheads, and minnows. Zander, carp, tench, and crucian carp are warm-water species

questions.question: Was ist Ursache für die Eutrophierung von Gewässern?

answers.B: Die Einleitung von Pumpwässern aus Torfstichen

questions.questionEn: What causes eutrophication?
questions.explanation: Eutrophication is specifically nutrient enrichment (primarily phosphorus and nitrogen) leading to increased biological productivity

questions.question: Sind Gewässer mit starker Wasserblüte durch Sauerstoffmangel gefährdet?

answers.A: Ja, ein massenhaftes Absterben der Algen führt zur Sauerstoffzehrung
answers.B: Nein, die Wasserblüte zeigt das Vorhandensein massenhafter junger Algen mit hoher Assimilation an
answers.C: Nein, die Wasserblüte entwickelt sich über dem Wasserspiegel durch See- und Teichrosen

questions.questionEn: Are waters with heavy algal blooms threatened by oxygen deficiency?

answersEn.A: Yes, mass algal die-off consumes oxygen
answersEn.B: No, the bloom indicates high assimilation
answersEn.C: No, the bloom develops from water lilies above the surface

questions.explanation: When algal blooms collapse, bacterial decomposition consumes enormous oxygen, often causing fish kills

questions.question: Was ist das Gelege aus fischereilicher Sicht?

answers.C: Die Gesamtzahl der sich im Nest eines Vogels befindlichen Eier

questions.questionEn: What is 'Gelege' in fisheries?
questions.explanation: In fisheries, Gelege refers to the vegetated littoral zone providing critical habitat and spawning areas

questions.question: Was sind oligotrophe Seen ?
questions.questionEn: What are oligotrophic lakes?
questions.explanation: Oligotrophic lakes have low nutrient concentrations, high clarity, and low productivity. Eutrophic are nutrient-rich

questions.question: Warum kann es im Winter bei geschlossener Eisund Schneedecke zur Ausstickung von stehenden Gewässern kommen?

answers.A: Weil kein natürlicher Sauerstoffeintrag sowie keine ausreichende Sauerstoffproduktion möglich ist
answers.B: Weil der Stickstoffgehalt zu hoch ist
answers.C: Weil die Temperaturen unter 6 C fallen und die Sauerstoffproduktion eingestellt wird

questions.questionEn: Why can winterkill occur under ice and snow cover?

answersEn.A: No oxygen input and insufficient production possible

questions.explanation: Ice and snow block both atmospheric oxygen diffusion and light for photosynthesis. Respiration depletes oxygen

questions.question: Die Reihenfolge des Pflanzenbewuchses vom Ufer aus ist wie folgt:
questions.questionEn: What is the correct plant zonation from shore outward?

answersEn.A: Reeds, floating-leaf plants, pondweeds, submerged meadows

questions.explanation: The classical zonation: emergent reeds, then floating-leaf plants, then submerged pondweeds, then deep-water meadows

questions.question: Weshalb ist für bestimmte Fischarten ein Mindestmaß notwendig?

answers.B: Um kleinere Fische vor dem Fangstress zu schonen
answers.C: Um eine ausreichende Nahrungsmenge für Raubfische im Gewässer zu belassen

questions.questionEn: Why are minimum sizes necessary?

answersEn.B: To spare small fish from catching stress
answersEn.C: To leave food for predatory fish

questions.explanation: Minimum sizes ensure fish spawn at least once before harvest, maintaining reproductive capacity. Not about stress or predator food

questions.question: Was versteht man unter einem mäandrierenden Fließgewässer?

answers.C: Ein Gewässer, das im Untergrund versickert

questions.questionEn: What is a meandering watercourse?

answersEn.A: A water body with many bends
answersEn.B: A water body with abundant bank vegetation
answersEn.C: A water body that seeps underground

questions.explanation: Meandering describes a river's natural sinuous pattern with alternating bends. Not about vegetation or groundwater

questions.question: Was ist ein Kolk ?
questions.questionEn: What is a 'Kolk' (scour pool)?

answersEn.A: A deep depression in a flowing water body

questions.explanation: A Kolk is a deep pool formed by water erosion on the outside of river bends, providing important deep-water fish refuge

questions.question: Warum stehen Fische im Fließgewässer gern hinter Hindernissen?

answers.C: Sie sind dort nur schwer zu beangeln

questions.questionEn: Why do fish stand behind obstacles in flowing water?

answersEn.C: They are hard to fish there

questions.explanation: Fish conserve energy by resting in reduced-flow areas behind obstacles. Hydrodynamic shelter is the primary benefit

questions.question: Welche ist die fischereilich vorteilhafteste Art der Wasserstandsregulierung in Fließgewässern?
questions.questionEn: What is the best water level regulation for fisheries?
questions.explanation: Free meandering maintains natural flow dynamics, sediment transport, and habitat diversity. Locks and weirs fragment habitats

questions.question: Was versteht man unter Ausstickung ?

answers.A: Das Fischsterben durch Sauerstoffmangel im Gewässer
answers.B: Eine Entfernung von Stickstoff aus dem Gewässer
answers.C: Das Ersticken der Fische an Land

questions.questionEn: What is 'Ausstickung' (winterkill)?
questions.explanation: Ausstickung is fish kill caused by oxygen depletion, especially under ice cover. Not nitrogen removal or terrestrial suffocation

questions.question: Was ist unter Verlandung zu verstehen?

answers.A: Die langsame Verflachung eines Gewässers durch Pflanzenwachstum sowie Sand- und Staubeintrag
answers.C: Die Überflutung von Landflächen durch Anstau von Wasser

questions.questionEn: What is siltation (Verlandung)?

answersEn.A: Gradual shallowing through plant growth and sediment
answersEn.C: Flooding of land by water impoundment

questions.explanation: Verlandung is natural lake succession: sediment accumulation and plant encroachment transforming open water to marsh and dry land

questions.question: Was versteht man unter einer Verbuttung?

answers.A: Schlecht ernährte und kaum noch wachsende Fischbestände mit großer Stückzahl

questions.questionEn: What is stunting (Verbuttung)?

answersEn.A: Poorly nourished, barely growing fish with high numbers

questions.explanation: Verbuttung describes overpopulated, stunted fish stocks with early maturity. Unrelated to flounders or aquaculture

questions.question: Warum können starke Ufergehölze die Produktivität von Gewässern beeinträchtigen?

answers.A: Sie behindern die Sonneneinstrahlung und verringern dadurch das Pflanzen- und Nährtieraufkommen
answers.B: Sie können Uferstrukturen zerstören und den Fischfang behindern
answers.C: Angelfischerei. Sie sind ein Hindernis für die Angelfischerei

questions.questionEn: Why can dense bank trees impair water productivity?

answersEn.A: They block sunlight, reducing plant and food organism growth
answersEn.B: They destroy bank structures and hinder fishing

questions.explanation: Excessive shading reduces photosynthesis by aquatic plants, decreasing primary production and the entire food web

questions.question: Welches sind typische Fischnährtiere der Bodenzone?
questions.questionEn: What are typical fish food organisms of the bottom zone?
questions.explanation: Red chironomid larvae (bloodworms) are the most important benthic food organisms. Water fleas are planktonic, backswimmers are pelagic

questions.question: Welche Arten gehören zu den Unterwasserpflanzen?
questions.questionEn: Which are true submerged aquatic plants?
questions.explanation: Pondweed, hornwort, and water milfoil are fully submerged. Water lilies are floating-leaf, reeds and sedges are emergent

questions.question: Zwischen welchen Fischarten besteht eine starke Nahrungskonkurrenz?
questions.questionEn: Between which fish is there strong food competition?
questions.explanation: Carp and bream are both benthic feeders competing for chironomid larvae and other bottom invertebrates
